{{Quote|Fool! These are chains. ''Mandalorian'' chains. They will hold even a Jedi prisoner.|[[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]|<ref name="TOTJaudio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Mandalorian manacles''' were a type of [[Binders/Legends|binders]] created by members of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] culture, who used nearly impervious [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] in their construction. The incredible strength afforded to the manacles by the iron [[Ore/Legends|ore]] allowed Mandalorian manacles to compete with the advent of newer binder technology for generations.

A popular export from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] in the [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to the creation of [[force shackles]], Mandalorian manacles were used by Mandalorians and foreigners alike. They became standard equipment for [[Mandalorian Guard/Legends|Mandalorian police]] units, and ''beskar''-made manacles were also favored by criminals and enemies of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] like the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] equipped his [[Gudb|henchmen]] with manacles to hold [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] captive, manacles used to bind [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]] during a [[Skirmish on Ambria|skirmish]] on [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]]; Thon was later freed when his [[Padawan/Legends|student]] [[Nomi Sunrider]] cut the chains with her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Millennia later, following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Imperial government requisitioned ''beskar'' equipment including Mandalorian manacles from the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] company. Mere [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] after, when Jedi Knight [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] came to the Mandalorians seeking aid in capturing her [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus]], Mandalorian manacles were recommended for his incarceration.

==Description==
{{Quote|We also wish to place orders for specialist equipment made from ''beskar'', including manacles, holding cages, security doors...|[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] requisition order to [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]]|<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>}}
[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] manacles were forged from [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]], a nearly indestructable<ref name="HotM" /> and rare [[Ore/Legends|ore]] found only on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>—the Mandalorians' cultural [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>—and Mandalore's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Concordia/Legends|Concordia]].<ref name="NewBattlefronts">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: New Battlefronts: The Visual Guide]]''</ref> Crafted by Mandalorian [[Metalsmith/Legends|metalsmiths]],<ref name="Sacrifice" /> ''beskar''-made manacles were a type of [[Binders/Legends|binders]] designed for restraining captives' [[Limb/Legends|wrists]] and ankles.<ref name="HotM" />

==History==
{{Quote|Because if you want to grab him, you're going to have to lure him into a trap, or injure him so badly that you can get ''beskar'' manacles on him.|Mandalorian soldier [[Goran Beviin]], discussing using Mandalorian manacles on [[Darth Caedus]] with his sister, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]|<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>}}
Created by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the ancient [[Mandalorian Crusaders]],<ref name="NomiSaga3" /> as knowledge of their imperious ''beskar'' composition spread in the [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to the creation of [[force shackles]] and [[energy cuffs]], Mandalorian manacles were a popular export from the planet Mandalore.<ref name="HotM" /> A set of Mandalorian manacles came into the ownership of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]], who outfitted his henchman [[Gudb]] with the manacles in order to incapacitate the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]].<ref name="TOTJaudio" /> Shortly after being captured and bound with the manacles, Thon was freed by his [[Padawan/Legends|student]], [[Nomi Sunrider]], who cut through the chains with her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="NomiSaga3" />

Even as more advanced binder technology developed in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], the strength of Mandalorian manacles allowed them to remain a competitor in the field. ''Beskar'' manacles established themselves as part of the standard equipment available to [[Mandalorian Guard/Legends|Mandalorian police]] units,<ref name="HotM" /> and they became known for their ability to [[Prison/Legends|imprison]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]]. Following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Imperial forces contacted the [[MandalMotors/Legends|MandalMotors]] company in Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], seeking to contract specialist equipment made of ''beskar'', with a shipment of Mandalorian manacles among the items the Empire requested.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> [[Standard decade/Legends|Decades]] later, in [[41 ABY]], the veteran Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Goran Beviin]] recommended Mandalorian manacles to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], who had come to Mandalore looking for assistance in capturing her recently-turned [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] brother, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]].<ref name="Revelation" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mandalorian manacles were first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in the [[Tales of the Jedi 5|fifth issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], part three of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider|''The Saga of Nomi Sunrider'']]. They were mentioned again in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]], and were featured in [[Abel G. Peña]]'s ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], published in the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 80|eightieth issue]] in [[March]] of [[2005]]. In [[2008]], Mandalorian manacles were mentioned in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novel [[Legacy of the Force: Revelation|''Revelation'']] by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]]. The following year, Traviss added another mention of the manacles in her ''[[Republic Commando]]'' novel ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'', published [[October 27]], [[2009]].
